blockingIssues.description: `agent` forwards nonexistent `<cwd>` paths to the bridge instead of rejecting them at the command layer, so VAL-CMD-017 is not satisfied.
`AgentBridge.spawn()` never registers its ephemeral scoop in orchestrator state during execution, so the required registered-scoop lifecycle is missing.
The no-override model path does not inherit the parent scoop/cone model in the real `ScoopContext`; it falls back to the global UI selection instead.

Publishing `globalThis.__slicc_agent` only in the offscreen document leaves the extension side-panel Terminal shell without a bridge because the panel and offscreen shells do not share window globals.
The allow-list wrapper still permits quoted command substitution/backticks such as `echo "$(curl ...)"`, so disallowed commands can be smuggled through an allowed head.
The allow-list wrapper does not parse or reject bare `&` and newline command separators, so disallowed commands can run after an allowed head in a single bash invocation.

The integration suite replaces the real `ScoopContext` path with a mocked `createContext` seam, so it does not actually validate the claimed full command → hook → bridge → scoop flow or real cone-tool bypass behavior.

The CLI-vs-extension parity scenario uses the same bootstrap helper for both labels, so it cannot catch divergence between `packages/webapp/src/ui/main.ts` and `packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen.ts`.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/environment.md` to direct workers to use `ws://localhost:5710/cdp` or discover the auto-allocated Chrome CDP port from the dev-server log instead of assuming port 9222.

Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to note that extension panel and offscreen shells do not share `globalThis`, so publishing `__slicc_agent` only in offscreen is insufficient for the side-panel Terminal.

Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to record that bridge-created scoops must be visible in orchestrator state during the run and that bash allow-list wrappers must handle full-shell separators and quoted command substitution conservatively.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature claims full-stack `agent` or scoop validation, require at least one test path that uses the real `ScoopContext`/tool assembly and only mocks the provider or transport seams. Do not treat a mocked `createContext` seam as sufficient evidence for model-threading or cone-tool-bypass behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `agent-bridge-implementation` and `core-integration-tests` passed with heavily mocked context factories while missing production-path bugs in model inheritance and full-stack scoop execution.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For CLI-vs-extension parity claims, require genuinely distinct harnesses or explicit spies on the runtime-specific bootstrap call sites (`ui/main.ts` vs `offscreen.ts`) rather than a shared helper that only varies by label.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `core-integration-tests` labeled two runs as CLI and extension parity, but both executions used the same `startBootstrap()` helper and never exercised the separate runtime call sites.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a separate webapp-worker skill note distinguishing "mock provider" from "mock `createContext`".
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the broader recommended skill update requiring at least one real `ScoopContext` path for full-stack `agent` validation.
